lineage: Life and Works of Rufinus with Jerome's Apology Against Rufinus.
Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.

A criticism on Rufinus' Apology to Anastasius. His excuses for not coming to Rome are absurd. His parents are dead and the journey is easy. No one ever heard before of his being imprisoned or exiled for the faith.

metadata.author: Life and Works of Rufinus with Jerome's Apology Against Rufinus

title: A criticism on Rufinus' Apology to Anastasius. His excuses for not coming to Rome are absurd. His parents are dead and the journey is easy. No one ever heard before of his being imprisoned or exiled for the faith.

source.title: NPNF2-03. Theodoret, Jerome, Gennadius, & Rufinus: Historical Writings

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: I will touch upon the other points, and set down the actual words of his letter:

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: “Although my faith was proved, at the time of the persecution by the heretics, when I was living in the holy church of Alexandria, by imprisonments and exiles, to which I was subjected because of the faith.”

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: I only wonder that he did not add “The prisoner of Jesus Christ,” or “I was delivered from the jaw of the lion,” or “I fought with beasts at Alexandria,” or “I have finished my course, I have kept the faith.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness.”

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: What exiles, what imprisonments are these which he describes?

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: I blush for this open falsehood.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: As if imprisonment and exile would be inflicted without judicial sentences!

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: I should like to have a list of these imprisonments and of the various provinces to which he tells us that he was forced into exile.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Next there appear to have been numerous imprisonments and an infinite number of exiles; so that he might at least name one of them all.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Let us have the acts of his confessorship produced, for hitherto we have been in ignorance of them; and so let us have the satisfaction of reciting his deeds with those of the other martyrs of Alexandria, and that he may be able to meet the people who bark against him with the words: “From henceforth let no man trouble me, for I bear in my body the marks of our Lord Jesus Christ.”
